In the opinion of Norton Rose Fulbright US LLP, San Francisco, California, Bond Counsel, under existing statutes, regulations, rulings and judicial 
decisions, and assuming compliance with certain covenants in the documents pertaining to the 2025ABC Bonds and requirements of the Internal Revenue 
Code of 1986, as described herein, interest on the 2025ABC Bonds is not included in the gross income of the owners thereof for federal income tax purposes.  
In the further opinion of Bond Counsel, interest on the 2025ABC Bonds is not treated as an item of tax preference for purposes of the federal alternative 
minimum tax on individuals. Bond Counsel is also of the opinion that, under existing law, interest on the 2025ABC Bonds is exempt from personal income 
taxes of the State of California. See “TAX MATTERS” herein.

This cover page contains certain information for quick reference only. It is not intended to be a summary of the security or terms of the water revenue bonds 
captioned above. Potential investors are instructed to read the entire Official Statement, including the appendices hereto, to obtain information essential 
to making an informed investment decision.
General. This Official Statement provides information regarding the above-captioned bonds (collectively, the “2025ABC Bonds”) of the Public Utilities 
Commission of the City and County of San Francisco (the “SFPUC”). Capitalized terms not otherwise defined on this cover shall have the meanings ascribed 
to such terms herein.
Authority for Issuance.  The SFPUC is issuing the 2025ABC Bonds pursuant to authority granted by the Charter of the City and County of San Francisco (the 
“City”). The 2025ABC Bonds will be issued under supplemental indentures that supplement the Amended and Restated Indenture, dated as of August 1, 2002 
(as amended and supplemented, the “Indenture”), by and between the SFPUC and U.S. Bank Trust Company, National Association, as successor to U.S. Bank 
National Association, as trustee (the “Trustee”). See “SECURITY FOR THE BONDS.”
Plan of Finance.   The 2025ABC Bonds are being issued to (i) pay the purchase price of certain outstanding water revenue bonds of the SFPUC tendered 
for cash pursuant to the Invitation, (ii) current refund certain outstanding water revenue bonds of the SFPUC, (iii) advance refund certain outstanding water 
revenue bonds of the SFPUC and (iv) pay the costs of issuance of the 2025ABC Bonds. See “PLAN OF REFUNDING” and “ESTIMATED SOURCES AND USES 
OF FUNDS.”
Denominations and Interest. The 2025ABC Bonds will be available in denominations of $5,000 or any integral multiple thereof and will mature in the years 
and amounts and accrue interest from their date of delivery at the rates set forth on the inside cover pages of this Official Statement. Interest on the 2025ABC 
Bonds is payable semiannually on May 1 and November 1 of each year, commencing November 1, 2025. See “THE 2025ABC BONDS.”
Book‑Entry Only. The 2025ABC Bonds will be issued as fully registered bonds, registered in the name of Cede & Co., as nominee of The Depository Trust 
Company (“DTC”), New York, New York, and will be available to ultimate purchasers (the “Beneficial Owners
”) under the book-entry only system maintained 
by DTC. Beneficial Owners will not receive physical certificates representing their interests in the 2025ABC Bonds. The principal of, premium, if any, and 
interest on the 2025ABC Bonds are payable to DTC by the Trustee, and, so long as DTC is acting as securities depository for the 2025ABC Bonds, disbursements 
of such payments to DTC Participants is the responsibility of DTC and disbursements of such payments to the Beneficial Owners is the responsibility of DTC 
Participants. See “THE 2025ABC BONDS.”
Redemption. The 2025ABC Bonds are subject to redemption prior to their stated maturities as described herein. See “THE 2025ABC BONDS – Redemption.”
Security. Under the Indenture, the SFPUC has irrevocably pledged the Revenues of its Water Enterprise and all Refundable Credits (in the case of Bonds 
issued as Build America Bonds) received by the SFPUC to the punctual payment of principal of, premium, if any, and interest on the 2025ABC Bonds and all 
outstanding parity revenue bonds, notes or other evidences of indebtedness authorized under the Indenture, subject to the allocation of funds provided in the 
Indenture. The 2025ABC Bonds are payable on parity with certain Outstanding Bonds previously issued by the SFPUC under the Indenture and all outstanding 
parity revenue bonds, notes or other evidences of indebtedness authorized under the Indenture. See “SECURITY FOR THE BONDS.”
Limited Obligation. The SFPUC is not obligated to pay the principal of, premium, if any, or interest on the 2025ABC Bonds except from 
Revenues of the Water Enterprise. The SFPUC has no taxing power. The General Fund of the City is not liable for the payment of the principal 
of, premium, if any, or interest on the 2025ABC Bonds, and neither the credit nor the taxing power of the City is pledged to the payment of 
the principal of, premium, if any, or interest on the 2025ABC Bonds. The 2025ABC Bonds are not secured by a legal or equitable pledge of, or 
charge, lien or encumbrance upon, any of the property of the City or of the SFPUC or any of its income or receipts, except Revenues of the Water 
Enterprise. The 2025ABC Bonds are not secured by the revenues of the SFPUC’s Wastewater Enterprise or Power Enterprise or the revenues 
of CleanPowerSF. See “SECURITY FOR THE BONDS.”

The 2025ABC Bonds are offered when, as and if issued by the SFPUC and received by the Underwriters, subject to the approval of validity by Norton Rose 
Fulbright US LLP, San Francisco, California, Bond Counsel to the SFPUC, and to certain other conditions. Certain matters will be passed upon for the SFPUC and 
the City by Orrick, Herrington & Sutcliffe LLP, San Francisco, California, Disclosure Counsel, and by the City Attorney of the City and County of San Francisco. 
Montague DeRose and Associates, LLC, Walnut Creek, California, and Backstrom McCarley Berry & Co., LLC, San Francisco, California, Co-Municipal Advisors 
to the SFPUC, assisted in the structuring of this financing. Certain matters will be passed upon for the Underwriters by Hawkins Delafield & Wood LLP, San 
Francisco, California. It is expected that the 2025ABC Bonds in fully registered form will be available for delivery in book -entry form through the facilities of 
DTC on or about April 17, 2025.

Figure 1-1 (Not to Scale) – The above map depicts the Hetch Hetchy Regional Water System, which delivers water approximately 160 miles from the Hetch Hetchy 
Reservoir to San Francisco. The Water Enterprise consists of over 1,235 miles of pipeline, 11 reservoirs, six groundwater wells, 11 storage tanks, 25 pump stations, 
eight hydropneumatic stations and 1 8 chlorination stations loca ted within the San  Francisco city limits and further comprises over 389 miles of pipeline, over 
74 miles of tunnels, 1 1 reservoirs, 13  groundwater wells, five pump stations, and three water treatment plants located outside of San  Francisco. For further 
description of the SFPUC’s Water System, see “THE WATER ENTERPRISE.” 
The 2025ABC Bonds are not secured by a legal or equitable pledge of, or charge, lien or encumbrance upon, any of the property of the SFPUC or any of its income 
or receipts, except Revenues. See “SECURITY FOR THE BONDS.”

Figure 1-2 (Not to Scale) – The above map depicts the Water Enterprise service area (not including the Groveland 
Community Service District) and the locations of the Wholesale Customers (as defined herein). The Water Enterprise 
serves water to approximately 2.7 million people in the San  Francisco Bay Area region , delivering approximately 
185 million gallons per day of water. For further description of the Wholesale Customers, see “THE WATER 
ENTERPRISE – Wholesale Deliveries.” 
The 2025ABC Bonds are not secured by a legal or equitable pledge of, or charge, lien or encumbrance upon, any of 
the property of any of the Wholesale Customers or any of their respective revenue, income or receipts. The 2025ABC 
Bonds are secured solely by the Revenues of the Water Enterprise as described herein. See “SECURITY F OR THE 
BONDS.”

GENERAL INFORMATION 
No dealer, broker, salesperson or other person has been authorized by the SFPUC to give any information or to make 
any representation other than those contained herein and, if given or made, such other information or representation 
must not be relied upon as having been authorized by the SFPUC. 
This Official Statement does not constitute an offer to sell or the solicitation of an offer to buy, nor shall there be any 
sale of the 2025ABC Bonds, by any person in any jurisdiction in which it is unlawful for such person to make such 
an offer, solicitation or sale. This Official Statement is not to be construed as a contract with the purchasers of the 
2025ABC Bonds. Any statement  made in this Official Statement involving any forecast or matter of estimates or 
opinion, whether or not expressly so stated, is intended solely as such and not as a representation of fact.  
The information set forth herein other than that provided by the SFPUC, although obtained from sources which are 
believed to be reliable, is not guaranteed as to accuracy or completeness. The information and expressions of opinion 
herein are subject to change without notice and neither delivery of this Official Statement nor any sale made hereunder 
shall, under any circumstances, create any implication that there has been no change in the affairs of the SFPUC or 
the City since the date hereof. 
The Underwriters have provided the following sentence for inclusion in this Of ficial Statement: The Underwriters 
have reviewed the information in this Official Statement in accordance with, and as part of, their responsibilities to 
investors under the federal securities laws as applied to the facts and circumstances of this transact ion, but the 
Underwriters do not guarantee the accuracy or completeness of such information. 
The City maintains a website at https://www.sf.gov/ and the SFPUC maintains a website at https://www.sfpuc.gov/. 
The City and the SFPUC also maintain social media accounts. In addition, certain information and reports found on 
other websites and other information and reports are referred to in this Official Statement. The websites are provided 
for convenience only and information and reports available on such websites, social media accounts and the other 
referenced information and reports, are not incorporated by reference into this Official Statement and should not be 
relied upon in making an investment in the 2025ABC Bonds. 
The issuance and sale of the 2025ABC  Bonds have not been registered under the Securities Act of 1933 in reliance 
upon the exemption provided thereunder by Section 3(a)(2) for the issuance and sale of municipal securities. 
IN CONNECTION WITH THE OFFE RING OF THE 2025ABC BONDS, THE UNDERWRITERS MAY 
OVERALLOT OR EFFECT TRANSACTIONS WHICH STABILIZE OR MAINTAIN THE MARKET PRICE OF 
THE 2025ABC BONDS AT LEVELS ABOVE THAT WHICH MIGHT OTHERWISE PREV AIL IN THE OPEN 
MARKET. SUCH STABILIZING, IF COMMENCED, MAY BE  DISCONTINUED AT ANY TIME. 
CONSEQUENTLY , THE MARKET PRICE PAID BY AN INVESTOR DURING THE STABILIZATION PERIOD 
MAY BE HIGHER THAN THE PREV AILING MARKET PRICE. 
This Official Statement is delivered for use in connection with the issuance, sale and delivery of the 2025ABC Bonds 
and may not be reproduced or used, in whole or in part, for any other purpose.

STATEMENTS ARE PREDICTIONS AND ARE SUBJECT TO KNOWN AND UNKNOWN RISKS AND 
UNCERTAINTIES. NO ASSURANCE CAN BE GIVEN THAT THE FUTURE RESULTS DISCUSSED HEREIN 
WILL BE ACHIEVED, AND ACTUAL RESULTS MAY DIFFER MATERIALLY FROM THE FORECASTS 
DESCRIBED HEREIN. IN THIS RESPECT, THE WORDS “ESTIMATE,” “PROJECT,” “ANTICIPATE,” 
“EXPECT,” “INTEND,” “BELIEVE” AND SIMILAR EXPRESSIONS ARE INTENDED TO IDENTIFY 
FORWA RD-LOOKING STATEMENTS. ALL PROJECTIONS, FORECASTS, ASSUMPTIONS, EXPRESSIONS 
OF OPINIONS, ESTIMATES AND OTHER FORWARD- LOOKING STATEMENTS ARE EXPRESSLY 
QUALIFIED IN THEIR ENTIRETY BY THE CAUTIONARY STATEMENTS SET FORTH IN THIS OFFICIAL 
STATEMENT. GIVEN THEIR UNCERTAINTY , INVESTORS ARE CAUTIONED NOT TO PLACE UNDUE 
RELIANCE ON SUCH STATEMENTS. -ii-

INTRODUCTION 
This Introduction is qualified in its entirety by reference to the more detailed information included and 
referred to elsewhere in this Official Statement. The offering of the hereinafter defined 2025ABC  Bonds to potential 
investors is made only by means of the entire Official Statement. Capitalized terms  used in this Introduction and not 
otherwise defined have the respective meanings assigned to them elsewhere in this Official Statement, including 
“APPENDIX A – SUMMARY OF CERTAIN PROVISIONS OF THE INDENTURE.” 
General 
This Official Statement, including the cover page and Appendices hereto, is provided to furnish certain 
information in connection with the offering by the Public Utilities Commission of the City and County of 
San Francisco (the “SFPUC”) of its San  Francisco Water Revenue Bonds, 2025 Series ABC, 2025 Sub- Series A 
(Refunding) (WSIP) (Green Bonds) (the “ 2025A Bonds”), San Francisco Water Revenue Bonds, 2025 Series ABC, 
2025 Sub-Series B (Refunding) (Regional Water and Local Water) (the “2025B Bonds”), and San  Francisco Water 
Revenue Bonds, 2025 Series ABC, 2025 Sub-Series C (Refunding) (Hetch Hetchy Water) (the “2025C Bonds” and, 
together with the 2025A Bonds and the 2025B Bonds, the “2025ABC Bonds”). 
In addition, the SFPUC anticipates that it will issue its San Francisco Water Revenue Bonds to finance 
and refinance a portion of the design, acquisition and construction of various capital projects of benefit to the 
Water Enterprise in June 2025 (collec tively, the “ 2025 New Money Bonds”) and its San Francisco Water 
Revenue Bonds to refund all or a portion of the SFPUC’s outstanding San Francisco Water Revenue Bonds 
issued as Build America Bonds as early as June 2025 (the “2025 BABs Refunding Bonds ”). The anticipated 
transactions are subject to approval by the SFPUC’s governing body (the “Commission”)  in May 2025. If 
approved by the Commission, the size, timing and structure of the anticipated transactions remain subject to 
market conditions and the SFPUC reserves the right to modify or cancel its plans as it deems appropriate. The 
2025 New Money Bonds  and the 2025 BABs Refunding Bonds  are not being offered pursuant to this Official 
Statement. 
Authority for Issuance 
The 2025ABC Bonds will be issued by the SFPUC pursuant to authority granted by Section 9.109 of the 
Charter (the “Charter”) of the City and County of San Francisco (the “City”) and an ordinance that was finally passed 
by the Board of Supervisors of the City (the “ Board of Supervisors ”) on June 18, 2024  (the “ Refunding 
Ordinance”).  In addition, the 2025ABC  Bonds will be issued under a resolution adopted by the SFPUC governing 
body (the “Commission”) on March 11, 2025. 
The 2025A Bonds will be issued under a Forty -Seventh Supplemental Indenture  (the “ Forty-Seventh 
Supplemental Indenture”), dated as of April 1, 2025, by and between the SFPUC and U.S. Bank Trust Company, 
National Association, as successor to U.S. Bank National Association, as trustee (the “Trustee”). The 2025B Bonds 
will be issued under  a Forty-Eighth Supplemental Indenture (the “Forty-Eighth Supplemental Indenture”), dated 
as of April 1, 2025, by and between the SFPUC and the Trustee.  The 2025C Bonds will be issued under a Forty-Ninth 
First Supplemental Indenture (the “ Forty-Ninth Supplemental Indenture ”), dated as of April 1 , 2025, by and 
between the SFPUC and the Trustee.  The Forty-Seventh Supplemental Indenture , the Forty-Eighth Supplemental 2 
Indenture and the Forty-Ninth Supplemental Indenture each supplement the Amended and Restated Indenture, dated 
as of August 1, 2002 (as amended and supplemented, the “Indenture”), by and between the SFPUC and the Trustee. 
See also “OBLIGATIONS PAYABLE FROM REVENUES – Authority for Issuance of Revenue Bonds and 
Other Obligations Payable from Revenues.” 
Plan of Refunding 
The 2025ABC Bonds are being issued to (i) pay the purchase price of certain outstanding water revenue 
bonds of the SFPUC tendered for cash pursuant to the Invitation (as defined herein), (ii) current refund certain 
outstanding water revenue bonds of the SFPUC , (iii) advance refund certain outstanding water revenue bonds of the 
SFPUC and (iv) pay the costs of issuance of the 2025ABC Bonds. See “PLAN OF REFUNDING” and “ESTIMATED 
SOURCES AND USES OF FUNDS.” 
The SFPUC and the Water Enterprise 
The SFPUC is a department of the City responsible for the maintenance, operation and development of three 
utility enterprises. See “THE PUBLIC UTILITIES COMMISSION.” 
The SFPUC owns and operates a municipal water supply, storage and distribution system (the “ Water 
Enterprise”) that provides drinking water to (i) retail customers in San Francisco and certain retail customers outside 
of San Francisco (collectively, the “Retail Customers”), (ii) 26 wholesale customer entities in San  Mateo, Alameda 
and Santa Clara counties that are members of the Bay Area Water Supply and Conservation Agency (“ BAWSCA”) 
pursuant to the hereinafter defined Water Supply Agreement and individual contractual agreements (collectively, the 
“Wholesale Customers ”), and (iii)  one additional wholesale customer, Cordilleras Mutual Water Company in 
San Mateo County (which is not a BAWSCA member or a party to the Water Supply Agreement), pursuant to an 
individual contractual agreement. The Water Enterprise consists of water treatment, storage and transmission facilities 
located outside of San  Francisco (the “Regional Water System ”) and water treatment , storage  and distribution 
facilities located inside San Francisco (the “In-City Distribution System”). See “WATER FACILITIES.” 
Water rates for Retail Customers are set by the SFPUC, subject to rejection by resolution of the Board of 
Supervisors. Retail water sales typically account for approximately 30% of total water sales, and revenues from such 
retail water sales generally account for approximately 50% of total water sales revenues. Water rates for Wholesale 
Customers are set pursuant to the Water Supply Agreement between the City and the Wholesale Custome rs, which 
became effective in 2009 and was most recently amended and restated in 2021 (the “Water Supply Agreement ” or 
“WSA”). Wholesale water sales typically account for approximately 70% of total water sales and revenues from such 
wholesale water sales generally account for approximately 50% of total water sales revenues . 
For more information regarding water sales, water sales revenues, and the WSA, see  “THE WATER 
ENTERPRISE,” “FINANCIAL OPERATIONS” and “APPENDIX B – SUMMARY OF CERTAIN PROVISIONS 
OF THE WATER SUPPLY AGREEMENT.” 
The SFPUC also provides wastewater services to customers in San  Francisco and to several wholesale 
customers outside of San Francisco (the “Wastewater Enterprise”), and power, mainly hydroelectric, for City 
government operations and to other users (the “Power Enterprise”), and manages a Community Choice Aggregation 
program (“CleanPowerSF”). The revenues of these other enterprises are not available for, and do not secure, 
payment of the principal of, premium, if any, or interest on the Bonds (as defined herein), including the 
2025ABC Bonds. 
SFPUC Water Supply 
The SFPUC’s primary water supply source is the Upper Tuolumne River watershed in the Sierra Nevada 
mountains. The amount of water available to the SFPUC is subject to prevailing hydrological conditions and may be 
limited during times of drought. The SFPUC has taken a variety of steps to enhance water supply reliability. See “THE 
WATER ENTERPRISE.” 3 
Security for the Bonds 
Under the Indenture, the SFPUC has irrevocably pledged the Revenues of its Water Enterprise and all 
Refundable Credits (in the case of Bonds issued as Build America Bonds) received by the SFPUC to the punctual 
payment of principal of, premium, if any, and interest on the 2025ABC Bonds and all outstanding parity revenue 
bonds, notes or other evidences of indebtedness authorized by the Indenture (collectively, the “Bonds”), subject to the 
allocation of funds provided in the Indenture. The 2025ABC Bonds are payable on parity with certain Outstanding 
Bonds previously issued by the SFPUC under the Indenture. 
The SFPUC is not obligated to pay the principal of, premium, if any, or interest on the 2025ABC Bonds 
except from Revenues of the Water Enterprise. The SFPUC has no taxing power. The General Fund of the City 
is not liable for the payment of the principal of, premium, if any, or interest on the 2025ABC Bonds, and neither 
the credit nor the taxing power of  the City is pledged to the payment of the principal of, premium, if any, or 
interest on the 2025ABC Bond s. The 2025ABC Bonds are not secured by a legal or equitable pledge of, or 
charge, lien or encumbrance upon, any of the property of the City or of the SFPUC or any of its income or 
receipts, except Revenues of the Water Enterprise. The 2025ABC Bonds are not secured by the revenues of the 
Wastewater Enterprise or the Power Enterprise or the revenues of CleanPowerSF. 
No Bond Reserve Account will be established for the 2025ABC Bonds. Bond Reserve Accounts have been 
established with respect to certain other series of Bonds, but such Bond Reserve Accounts do not secure the 2025ABC 
Bonds. 
See “SECURITY FOR THE BONDS.” 
Outstanding and Future Parity Bonds and Other Indebtedness 
The SFPUC has previously issued Bonds, and in the future expects to issue additional Bonds, pursuant to the 
Indenture and secured by Revenues of the Water Enterprise on parity with the 2025ABC Bonds. See “OBLIGATIONS 
PAYABLE FROM REVENUES.” 
The Indenture provides that additional series of Bonds secured by Revenues on parity with the Outstanding 
Bonds and the 2025ABC Bonds (each an “Additional Series of Bonds”) may be issued if certain conditions are met. 
See “SECURITY FOR THE BONDS – Additional Series of Bonds.” 
The SFPUC may issue additional Bonds from time to time to fund additional capital projects (see 
“FINANCING OF CAPITAL IMPROVEMENTS”) and may also issue refunding Bonds (see “SECURITY FOR THE 
BONDS – Refunding Bonds”). 
Amendments Effected by Forty-Third Supplemental Indenture 
The Forty-Third Supplemental Indenture (the “Forty-Third Supplemental Indenture”), dated as of July 1, 
2023, by and between the SFPUC and the Trustee, amends and supplements certain provisions of the Indenture, 
including provisions relating to the calculation of “Maximum Annual Debt Service,” “Annual Debt Service,” “Average 
Annual Debt Service,” the incurrence of “Parity Loans,” the conditions under which Additional Series of Bonds may 
be issued, the filing of annual audited financial information with the Trustee and the rate covenants. The provisions of 
the Forty-Third Supplemental Indenture will become effective when the written consents of the Owners of a majority 
in aggregate amount of the Bond Obligation of the Bonds then Outstanding and of each Credit Provider, if any, have 
been filed with the Trustee and certain other requirements under the Indenture have been satisfied. Approximately 
19.5% of the aggregate amount of the Bond Obligation of the Bonds currently Outstanding have been obtained.  By 
their purchase of 2025ABC Bonds, the purchasers of 2025ABC Bonds irrevocably agree to, accept and consent 
to the provisions of such amendments . Upon the issuance of the 2025ABC Bonds, approximately 32.8% of the 
aggregate amount of the Bond Obligation of the Bonds then Outstanding will have been obtained. See “PROPOSED 
AMENDMENTS TO INDENTURE” and “APPENDIX A – SUMMARY OF CERTAIN PROVISIONS OF THE 
INDENTURE – FORTY-THIRD SUPPLEMENTAL INDENTURE – PROPOSED AMENDMENTS TO  4 
INDENTURE” for a mo re detailed explanation of the amendments effected by the Forty- Third Supplemental 
Indenture. 
Risk Factors 
Investment in the 2025ABC Bonds is subject to certain risks that should be considered, in addition to other 
matters set forth in this Official Statement, in evaluating an investment in the 2025ABC Bonds. For a general overview 
of certain risk factors, see “RISK FACTORS.” 
Continuing Disclosure 
The SFPUC will covenant for the benefit of the Owners and Beneficial Owners of the 2025AB C Bonds to 
provide certain financial information and operating data regarding the Water Enterprise not later than March 31 
following the end of its Fiscal Year (presently June  30), beginning on March 31, 2026, with respect to the report for 
Fiscal Year 2024-25, and to provide notices of the occurrence of certain enumerated events. These covenants will be 
made to assist the Underwriters in complying with Rule  15c2-12(b)(5) of the Securities and Exchange Commission 
(the “Rule”). See “CONTINUING DISCLOSURE” and “APPENDIX E – FORM OF CONTINUING DISCLOSURE 
CERTIFICATE.” 
Other Matters 
Brief descriptions of the 2025ABC Bonds, the security and sources of payment for the 2025ABC Bonds, the 
SFPUC, the Water Enterprise, the SFPUC’s capital improvement program are provided herein. Such descriptions do 
not purport to be comprehensive or definitive. Definitions of certain capitalized terms used herein may be found in 
“APPENDIX A – SUMMARY OF CERTAIN PROVISIONS OF THE INDENTURE.” All references made to various 
documents herein are qualified in t heir entirety by reference to the forms thereof, all of which are available for 
inspection at the office of the SFPUC at:

THE 2025ABC BONDS 
General 
The 2025ABC Bonds will be dated as of their date of delivery and will accrue interest from their date of 
delivery at the rates per annum set forth on the inside cover pages of this Official Statement. Interest on the 2025ABC 
Bonds will be calculated on the basis of a 360-day year consisting of twelve 30-day months. Interest on the 2025ABC 
Bonds is payable on May 1 and November 1 of each year, beginning November 1, 2025. 
The 2025ABC Bonds will mature on the dates and in the principal amounts set forth on the inside cover 
pages of this Official Statement. The 2025ABC  Bonds will be issued in fully registered form in denominations of 
$5,000 or any integral multiple of $5,000. 
Under the Indenture, the record date with respect to the payment of principal of and interest on the 2025ABC 
Bonds is the 15th day of the calendar month immediately preceding an interest payment date, whether or not such day 
is a Business Day. 
  5 
Securities Depository and Book-Entry Only System 
The 2025ABC Bonds will be registered in the name of Cede & Co., as nominee for The Depository Trust 
Company (“DTC”), New York, New York, as the Owner of the 2025ABC  Bonds. So long as DTC, or its nominee, 
Cede & Co., is the Owner of the 2025ABC  Bonds, all payments on the 2025ABC  Bonds will be made directly to 
DTC. Disbursement of such payments to the DTC Participants will be the responsibility of DTC, and disbursement of 
such payments to the Beneficial Owners of the 2025ABC Bonds will be the responsibility of the DTC Participants. 
See “APPENDIX F – SECURITIES DEPOSITORY AND THE BOOK-ENTRY SYSTEM.” 
Redemption 
Optional Redemption of 2025A Bonds. The 2025A Bonds maturing on or after November 1, 2035 are subject 
to redemption prior to their stated maturity, at the option of the SFPUC, from any source of available funds, as a whole 
or in part, on any date on or after May 1, 2035 from such maturities or portions of maturities as are determined by the 
SFPUC and (except as otherwise provided under the procedures of Securities Depository for the 2025A Bonds) by lot 
within any one maturity, at a redemption price equal to 100% of the principal amount of the 2025A  Bonds to be 
redeemed, plus accrued interest thereon to the date fixed for redemption, without premium.  
Optional Redemption of 2025B Bonds. The 2025B Bonds maturing on or after November 1, 2036 are subject 
to redemption prior to their stated maturity, at the option of the SFPUC, from any source of available funds, as a whole 
or in part, on any date on or after May 1, 2035 from such maturities or portions of maturities as are determined by the 
SFPUC and (except as otherwise provided under the procedures of Securities Depository for the 2025B Bonds) by lot 
within any one maturity, at a redemption price equal to 100% of the principal amount of the 2025B  Bonds to be 
redeemed, plus accrued interest thereon to the date fixed for redemption, without premium.  
Optional Redemption of 2025C Bonds. The 2025C Bonds maturing on or after November 1, 2036 are subject 
to redemption prior to their stated maturity, at the option of the SFPUC, from any source of available funds, as a whole 
or in part, on any date on or after May 1, 2035, from such maturities or portions of maturities as are determined by the 
SFPUC and (except as otherwise provided under the procedures of Securities Depository for the 2025C Bonds) by lot 
within any one maturity, at a redemption price equal to 100% of the principal amount of the 2025C  Bonds to be 
redeemed, plus accrued interest thereon to the date fixed for redemption, without premium. 
Selection of 2025ABC Bonds for Redemption. Subject to DTC’s procedures relating to the selection of bonds 
for redemption described above, whenever less than all of the 2025ABC  Bonds, of any one tenor and maturity are 
called for redemption and those 2025ABC  Bonds are redeemable by lot, the Trustee will select the 2025ABC  Bonds 
of the tenor and maturity to be redeemed from the Outstanding 2025ABC  Bonds of that tenor and maturity, by lot or 
by any other manner the Trustee deems fair and equitable. For purposes of such selection, 2025ABC  Bonds will be 
deemed to be made up of $5,000 portions of principal, any of which may be redeemed separately. 
Notice of Redemption. Notice of redemption will be mailed by the Trustee at least 30 days but not more than 
60 days prior to the redemption date, to DTC (so long as DTC’s book-entry system is used). The actual receipt by the 
owner of any 2025ABC Bond of notice of such redemption is not a condition precedent to redemption, and failure to 
receive a redemption notice or any defect in a redemption notice will not affect the validity of the proceedings for the 
redemption of such 2025ABC Bond or the cessation of the accrual of interest on the date fixed for such redemption. 
See “APPENDIX F – SECURITIES DEPOSITORY AND THE BOOK-ENTRY SYSTEM.” 
Rescission of Notice of Redemption. The SFPUC may, at its option, prior to the date fixed for redemption in 
any notice of optional redemption, rescind and cancel such notice of redemption by written request to the Trustee and 
the Trustee will mail notice of such cancellation to the recipients of the notice of redemption being cancelled. 
Effect of Redemption. When notice of redemption has been duly given as described above, and moneys for 
payment of the redemption price are held by the Trustee, the 2025ABC  Bonds called for redemption will, on the 
redemption date designated in such notice, become due and payable at the redemption price specified in such notice; 
and from and after the date so designated interest on such 2025ABC Bonds called for redemption will cease to accrue, 
and such 2025ABC Bonds will cease to be entitled to any benefit or security under the Indenture, and the Owners of 6 
said 2025ABC Bonds will have no rights in respect thereof except to receive payment of the redemption price thereof. 
The Trustee, upon surrender for payment of any of said 2025ABC Bonds will pay such 2025ABC  Bonds at the 
applicable redemption price, together with accrued interest thereon. All 2025ABC Bonds redeemed will be cancelled 
upon surrender and no 2025ABC Bonds will be issued in place thereof. 
Defeasance 
The obligations of the SFPUC and the pledge, lien, covenants and agreements of the SFPUC made or 
provided for in the Indenture will be fully discharged and satisfied as to any 2025ABC  Bond and will no longer be 
deemed outstanding thereunder if certain conditions set forth in the Indenture are satisfied. See “ APPENDIX A – 
SUMMARY OF CERTAIN PROVISIONS OF THE INDENTURE – DEFEASANCE.” 
PLAN OF REFUNDING 
General 
On March 17, 2025, the SFPUC, with the assistance of Jefferies LLC (the “ Dealer Manager”), released an 
Invitation to Tender Bonds for Purchase (the “ Invitation”) inviting beneficial owners of certain maturities ( such 
maturities, the “Target Bonds”) of the SFPUC’s outstanding San Francisco Water Revenue Bonds, 2016 Series AB, 
2016 Sub-Series A (Refunding) (the “2016A Bonds”), San Francisco Water Revenue Bonds, 2017 Series DEFG, 2017 
Sub-Series D (Refunding) (Green Bonds) (the “ 2017D Bonds”), San Francisco Water Revenue Bonds, 2019 Series 
ABC, 2019 Sub- Series A (Refunding – Federally Taxable) (WSIP) (Green Bonds) (the “ 2019A Bonds ”), 
2019 Sub-Series B (Refunding – Federally Taxable) (Hetch Hetchy) (the “ 2019B Bonds”), and 2019 Sub- Series C 
(Refunding – Federally Taxable) (Local Water Main) (the “ 2019C Bonds”), San Francisco Water Revenue Bonds, 
Series 2020EFGH, 2020 Sub-Series E (Refunding – Federally Taxable) (WSIP) (Green Bonds) (the “2020E Bonds”), 
2020 Sub- Series F (Refunding – Federally Taxable) (Non -WSIP) (the “ 2020F Bonds ”), 2020 Sub- Series G 
(Refunding – Federally Taxable) (Local Water) (the “2020G Bonds”), and 2020 Sub-Series H (Refunding – Federally 
Taxable) (Hetch Hetchy Water) (the “2020H Bonds”) to tender such Bonds for purchase pursuant to the terms of the 
Invitation. The invitation to tender under the Invitation expired by its terms on March 31, 2025. On April 2, 2025, the 
SFPUC accepted tenders of the Target Bonds shown in the tables set forth under “ – Refunded Bonds – Purchased 
Bonds and Advance Refunded Bonds” below, subject to the conditions set forth in the Invitation.  Target Bonds validly 
tendered and accepted for purchase (collectively, the “ Purchased Bonds ”) will be purchased on April 17, 202 5, 
subject to the conditions set forth in the Invitation, and cancelled. 
Concurrently with purchasing the Purchased Bonds, the SFPUC will advance refund  additional 2019A 
Bonds, 2020F Bonds, 2020G Bonds and 2020H Bonds (such maturities  are collectively referred to herein as  the 
“Advance Refunded Bonds”), as deemed necessary by the SFPUC to comply with applicable tax law.   
The 2025ABC Bonds are also being issued to refund all of the SFPUC’s outstanding San Francisco Water 
Revenue Bonds, 2015 Series A (Refunding) (the “2015A Bonds”), San Francisco Water Revenue Bonds, 2017 Series 
ABC, 2017 Sub-Series A (WSIP) (Green Bonds) (the “2017A Bonds”), 2017 Sub-Series B (Non-WSIP) (the “2017B 
Bonds”), and 2017 Sub Series C (Hetch Hetchy) (the “2017C Bonds”).  Such refunded 2015A Bonds will be redeemed 
on May 17, 2025, and such refunded 2017A Bonds, 2017B Bonds and 2017C Bonds will be redeemed on April 18, 
2025. 
The proceeds of the 2025A Bonds will be applied to (i) refund by means of tender, purchase and cancellation 
certain Purchased Bonds, (ii) advance refund certain Advance Refunded Bonds, (iii) current refund all of the 2015A 
Bonds (such portion, the “Refunded 2015A Bonds”) and all of the 2017A Bonds (such portion, the “Refunded 2017A 
Bonds”) and (iv) pay the costs of issuance of the 2025A Bonds. 
The proceeds of the 2025B Bonds will be applied to (i) refund by means of tender, purchase and cancellation 
certain Purchased Bonds, (ii) advance refund certain Advance Refunded Bonds, (iii) current refund all of the 2017B 
Bonds (such portion, the “Refunded 2017B Bonds”) and (iv) pay the costs of issuance of the 2025B Bonds. 7 
The proceeds of the 2025C Bonds will be applied to (i) refund by means of tender, purchase and cancellation 
certain Purchased Bonds, (ii) advance refund certain Advance Refunded Bonds, (iii) current refund all of the 2017C 
Bonds (such portion, the “Refunded 2017C Bonds”) and (iv) pay the costs of issuance of the 2025C Bonds.

Escrow Fund 
A portion of the proceeds of the 2025ABC  Bonds in an amount sufficient to refund and legally defease the 
Current Refunded Bonds and the Advance Refunded Bonds will be deposited in an Escrow Fund established by the 
Trustee, acting as escrow agent (the “Escrow Agent ”), under an Escrow Agreement, dated as of April 1, 2025 (the 
“Escrow Agreement”), by and between the SFPUC and the Escrow Agent. A portion of the funds deposited in the 
Escrow Fund will be invested in certain securities, the principal of and interest on which, when received, will be 
sufficient, together with other available amounts held in the Escrow Fund, to pay the principal of and interest on the 
Current Refunded Bonds  and the Advance Refunded Bonds to be refunded on their respective redemption dates. 
Pursuant to the Indenture and the irrevocable instructions in the Escrow Agreement, the Current Refunded Bonds and 
the Advance Refunded Bonds to be refunded will be redeemed on their respective redemption dates at a redemption 
price equal to the principal amount of such Bonds, plus accrued interest to the redemption date, without premium. As 
a result of the deposit and application of funds as provided in the Escrow Agreement, the Current Refunded Bonds  
and the Advance Refunded Bonds will be defeased pursuant to the Indenture as of the date of issuance of the 2025ABC 
Bonds.   
Sufficiency of the deposits in the Escrow Fund for the purposes described in the previous paragraph will be 
verified by AW Smith LLC, Rockford, Minnesota. See “VERIFICATION OF MATHEMATICAL ACCURACY .”
